questions: Rendering approach: Effect layers need to wrap all lower-track content. Two options: (A) Flatten all lower tracks into a single offscreen composition per time range, then wrap with effect — simpler but may have performance implications with many tracks. (B) Apply CSS/WebGL effects at the track level without re-compositing — faster but limits effect types to CSS-compatible ones. Which approach do you prefer, or should we start with (A) as the general solution?
Should effect-layer clips support entrance/exit effects on themselves (e.g., the effect fades in/out), or should they only apply the continuous effect with hard cut boundaries?
Should effect-layer clips be allowed on any visual track, or should there be a dedicated 'effect track' type? A dedicated type would simplify the UI but reduce flexibility.

For the 'Add Effect Layer' button — where should it live? Options: (A) Timeline toolbar alongside existing buttons, (B) A '+' button on track headers, (C) Only via drag from the effect creator panel, (D) Right-click context menu on the timeline.
Should effect layers affect text clips on lower tracks, or only media/hold clips? Text clips are often used as overlays and users might not want effects applied to their text.
success_criteria: New `'effect-layer'` ClipType is recognized throughout the system without errors
Effect-layer clips can be created via UI (button or drag) and placed on visual tracks
Effect-layer clips render correctly in Remotion preview — the continuous effect visually applies to all clips on tracks below the effect layer for its duration
Multiple stacked effect layers compose correctly (top layer's effect wraps everything below including other effect layers' output)
Effect-layer clips have distinctive visual appearance in the timeline (different icon, semi-transparent/colored background)
Properties panel shows only relevant controls for effect-layer clips (continuous effect selector, intensity, params, timing)
Effect-layer clips can be resized, moved, and split like other clips
Save → reload round-trip preserves effect-layer clips with all their settings
Effect-layer clips on audio tracks are rejected/prevented
Partial time overlaps work correctly — the effect only applies during the effect-layer clip's duration, not before or after
assumptions: Reuse the existing `continuous` field on TimelineClip for the effect-layer's effect config rather than adding new fields — this minimizes type changes and leverages existing parameter/intensity infrastructure
Effect-layer clips use `hold` for duration (same as static image clips) since they have no source media with from/to
Only continuous effects are applicable to effect layers (not entrance/exit, which are per-clip animations)

The Remotion rendering approach will use React composition (wrapping lower tracks in effect components) rather than offscreen canvas or WebGL — this keeps the implementation within the existing Remotion pattern
No database migration is needed — the config JSONB column already stores whatever shape TimelineConfig has, and the effects table doesn't need changes
Default effect-layer clip duration is 5 seconds (same as other default clip durations in the codebase)
Custom effects created in the EffectCreatorPanel with category 'continuous' are eligible for use in effect layers
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
